title: Walks
lean_code_with_comments:
inductive EdgeStep (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | forward  {u v : Node} (h : G.tuh u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | backward {u v : Node} (h : G.hut u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | bidir    {u v : Node} (h : G.huh u v) : EdgeStep G u v

inductive Walk (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | nil  {v : Node} (hv : v ∈ G) : Walk G v v
  | cons {u v w : Node} (e : EdgeStep G u v) (p : Walk G v w) : Walk G u w

def Walk.support {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → List Node
  | .nil _ => [u]
  | .cons _ p => u :: p.support

def Walk.length {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → ℕ
  | .nil _ => 0
  | .cons _ p => p.length + 1

def Walk.intoV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => True

def Walk.outOfV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False

def Walk.intoVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastHeadAtRight e p

def Walk.outOfVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastTailAtRight e p

def Walk.IsPath {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} (p : Walk G u w) : Prop :=
  p.support.Nodup

def Walk.IsDirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.forward _) p => p.IsDirectedWalk
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False

def Walk.IsBidirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) p => p.IsBidirectedWalk
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False

def Walk.IsColliderWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.nil _) => True
  | .cons (.forward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.backward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.forward _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.backward _) (.cons _ _) => False

inductive HingeKind (G : CDMG Node) (u v : Node) : Type where
  | directed (h : G.hut u v) : HingeKind G u v
  | bidir    (h : G.huh u v) : HingeKind G u v

structure Bifurcation (G : CDMG Node) (v w : Node) where
  /-- LN's `v_{k-1}` — the right end of the left arm, sitting on the
      `apexL`-side of the hinge edge. -/
  apexL : Node
  /-- LN's `v_k` — the left end of the right arm, sitting on the
      `apexR`-side of the hinge edge.  When the hinge is directed
      (`.directed`), this is the LN's "source". -/
  apexR : Node
  /-- The left arm of the bifurcation, oriented to match the LN's
      "directed walk from $v_{k-1}$ to $v_0$" reading. -/
  leftArm : Walk G apexL v
  /-- The left arm is a directed walk (LN: "the subwalk
      $v_0 \hut \cdots v_{k-1}$ is a directed walk from $v_{k-1}$ to
      $v_0$"). -/
  hLeftArmDir : leftArm.IsDirectedWalk
  /-- The right arm of the bifurcation, oriented as the LN's "directed
      walk from $v_k$ to $v_n$". -/
  rightArm : Walk G apexR w
  /-- The right arm is a directed walk (LN: "the subwalk
      $v_k \tuh \cdots v_n$ is a directed walk from $v_k$ to $v_n$"). -/
  hRightArmDir : rightArm.IsDirectedWalk
  /-- The hinge edge `v_{k-1} \hus v_k`, encoded as a `HingeKind`
      to record *which* edge kind was chosen (rather than the
      `Prop`-irrelevant `Or` of `G.hus`).  `.directed` makes the
      bifurcation *directed-hinged* (the LN's "source" case);
      `.bidir` makes it *bidirected-hinged* (no source). -/
  hinge : HingeKind G apexL apexR
  /-- LN: "such that $v \ne w$". -/
  hVneW : v ≠ w
  /-- LN: "the walk contains $v$ exactly once".  Encoded as: `v` is
      not in the right arm's support, and appears exactly once in the
      left arm's support (necessarily at the right end of `leftArm`,
      since `leftArm` ends at `v`). -/
  hVOnce : v ∉ rightArm.support ∧ leftArm.support.count v = 1
  /-- LN: "the walk contains $w$ exactly once" (dual to `hVOnce`). -/
  hWOnce : w ∉ leftArm.support ∧ rightArm.support.count w = 1
  /-- LN addition `[bifurcation_right_chain_trivial_is_just_directed_
      walk]`: `w` has an arrowhead pointing towards it.  Either the
      right arm is non-trivial (then its last `.forward` step
      supplies the arrowhead automatically) or the right arm is
      trivial AND the *hinge itself* is bidirected (`.bidir`, head
      at both ends).  `hinge.isBidir` — not `G.huh apexL apexR` —
      is the load-bearing predicate here: see the design block
      above `HingeKind` for the verified bug a `G.huh apexL apexR`
      spelling produces in the coexistence regime, where a
      stand-alone bidirected edge between the apex pair would
      satisfy `G.huh apexL apexR` without actually being the
      hinge.  The dual condition for `v` is automatic from
      `hLeftArmDir` + the LN form of `hinge` — see the design
      comment above for why no `hArrowV` field is needed. -/
  hArrowW : rightArm.length > 0 ∨ hinge.isBidir

def Bifurcation.source {G : CDMG Node} {v w : Node}
    (b : Bifurcation G v w) : Option Node :=
  match b.hinge with
  | .directed _ => some b.apexR
  | .bidir _    => none
lean_code_without_comments:
inductive EdgeStep (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | forward  {u v : Node} (h : G.tuh u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | backward {u v : Node} (h : G.hut u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | bidir    {u v : Node} (h : G.huh u v) : EdgeStep G u v

inductive Walk (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | nil  {v : Node} (hv : v ∈ G) : Walk G v v
  | cons {u v w : Node} (e : EdgeStep G u v) (p : Walk G v w) : Walk G u w

def Walk.support {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → List Node
  | .nil _ => [u]
  | .cons _ p => u :: p.support

def Walk.length {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → ℕ
  | .nil _ => 0
  | .cons _ p => p.length + 1

def Walk.intoV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => True

def Walk.outOfV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False

def Walk.intoVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastHeadAtRight e p

def Walk.outOfVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastTailAtRight e p

def Walk.IsPath {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} (p : Walk G u w) : Prop :=
  p.support.Nodup

def Walk.IsDirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.forward _) p => p.IsDirectedWalk
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False

def Walk.IsBidirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) p => p.IsBidirectedWalk
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False

def Walk.IsColliderWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.nil _) => True
  | .cons (.forward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.backward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.forward _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.backward _) (.cons _ _) => False

inductive HingeKind (G : CDMG Node) (u v : Node) : Type where
  | directed (h : G.hut u v) : HingeKind G u v
  | bidir    (h : G.huh u v) : HingeKind G u v

structure Bifurcation (G : CDMG Node) (v w : Node) where

  apexL : Node

  apexR : Node

  leftArm : Walk G apexL v

  hLeftArmDir : leftArm.IsDirectedWalk

  rightArm : Walk G apexR w

  hRightArmDir : rightArm.IsDirectedWalk

  hinge : HingeKind G apexL apexR

  hVneW : v ≠ w

  hVOnce : v ∉ rightArm.support ∧ leftArm.support.count v = 1

  hWOnce : w ∉ leftArm.support ∧ rightArm.support.count w = 1

  hArrowW : rightArm.length > 0 ∨ hinge.isBidir

def Bifurcation.source {G : CDMG Node} {v w : Node}
    (b : Bifurcation G v w) : Option Node :=
  match b.hinge with
  | .directed _ => some b.apexR
  | .bidir _    => none
lean_explanation:
The formalisation parameterises everything over an ambient vertex type `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]`. The decidable-equality instance is needed for `List.count` (used in the bifurcation's "exactly once" endpoint constraints) and `List.Nodup` (which encodes the notion of a path).

The notes' symbol $a_k$ — "the individual edge of a walk" — is reified as a `Type _`-valued ADT `EdgeStep G u v`, with three constructors `forward` / `backward` / `bidir` carrying the corresponding `G.tuh` / `G.hut` / `G.huh` proofs. The two indices `u v : Node` are the endpoints of the edge as walked: `u` is where the step starts, `v` where it ends, so `backward` means "traversed against the underlying directed-edge orientation".

The main inductive `Walk G u w` mirrors Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.Walk`: `nil` is the trivial single-vertex walk (carrying an explicit membership proof `hv : v ∈ G`, matching the notes' $v_0 \in G$), and `cons (e : EdgeStep G u v) (p : Walk G v w)` prepends one edge step onto a tail walk. The type-level chaining $u \to v \to w$ makes the matching condition between consecutive edges a free unification at type-checking time rather than an explicit proof obligation.

The walk-level helpers `Walk.support` and `Walk.length` are recursive: `support` returns a `List Node` of the vertex sequence (preserving order and multiplicity, since the notes explicitly allow repetition), and `length` counts the `cons` constructors — i.e. the notes' $n$.

Items 2–4 (`IsDirectedWalk`, `IsBidirectedWalk`, `IsColliderWalk`) and item 5 (`IsPath`) are encoded as `Prop`-valued predicates on `Walk`, not as fresh inductive types. `IsPath` is literally `p.support.Nodup`, reusing Mathlib's standard duplicate-free list predicate together with all of its decidability and permutation infrastructure.

For item 6, `HingeKind G u v` is a 2-constructor inductive (`directed` carrying `G.hut u v`, `bidir` carrying `G.huh u v`). `Bifurcation G v w` is a `structure` with named fields for the two apex vertices, the two directed sub-walks and their directedness proofs, the hinge, distinctness, single-occurrence constraints on the endpoints, and an arrowhead-at-$v_n$ constraint. `Bifurcation.source` returns `Option Node` by pattern-matching on the hinge kind, capturing the notes' partial "if the hinge is directed then the bifurcation has source $v_k$".
design_choices:
**The walk's individual edge is a `Type`-valued algebraic data type, not a propositional disjunction.** A `Prop`-side $(u,v) \in G.E \,\vee\, (v,u) \in G.E \,\vee\, (u,v) \in G.L$ would erase which disjunct fired and force every downstream pattern match to peer through `Or.elim`. Giving `EdgeStep` three constructors (`forward` / `backward` / `bidir`) lets the four into/out classifications and the three specialised walk predicates pattern-match directly on the notes' $\hus / \tuh / \suh / \hut / \huh$ distinctions. A second alternative — bake the three cases into `Walk.cons` as three constructors — was rejected because it would triple-case-split every walk manipulation and lose the "edge" abstraction needed by the bifurcation's hinge.

**The specialised walk variants are predicates on `Walk`, not separate inductive types.** This lets the next definition's "$\exists$ directed walk" existentials map verbatim to `∃ p : Walk G w v, p.IsDirectedWalk` with no coercion, and reuses `Walk.support` / `Walk.length` on every variant.

**The trivial walk vacuously satisfies all three specialised walk types.** The notes explicitly admit $n \ge 0$ in items 2–4; a commented-out exclusion line in the source ("Directed walks exclude the trivial walk per definition.") shows the author considered and rejected exclusion. Consumers needing a non-trivial witness (e.g. the acyclicity definition) add `0 < p.length` at the use site.

**For $n=1$, a collider walk requires a bidirected edge.** The notes' formal pattern $v_0 \suh v_1 + v_{n-1} \hus v_n$ at $n=1$ collapses onto a single edge that must carry arrowheads at both endpoints. The inline note "for $n=1$ this reads: $v \sus w$" is read in this stricter sense; purely directed edges $v \tuh w$ or $v \hut w$ are excluded.

**The bifurcation's hinge is the data type `HingeKind`, not the propositional disjunction `G.hut ∨ G.huh`.** This is the load-bearing decision for the bifurcation file. Because the underlying CDMG definition admits a coexistence regime — a directed edge from `apexR` to `apexL` AND a bidirected edge between the same pair can both inhabit the graph — a proof-irrelevant `Or` would lose the constructor information needed by `Bifurcation.source` to identify the *chosen* hinge kind. Pattern-matching on a `Type`-level ADT records which constructor was actually selected.

**The bifurcation is a `structure`, not an existential predicate.** Downstream consumers manipulate bifurcations as data: the alternative-characterisation claim extracts the source field, and chapter-11 confounding theory splits at the hinge under marginalisation. A `Prop`-side `∃ walk, …` would force every consumer to destructure to recover the arms.

**The arrowhead-at-$v_n$ constraint reads off the hinge constructor, not the wider edge sets.** The form requires $v_n$ to have an arrowhead; this is automatic when the right arm is non-trivial (the last forward step's head sits at $v_n$), but with a trivial right arm only the chosen hinge can supply it. Keying the check on "does some bidirected edge between the apex pair exist in $L$" would let an unrelated bidirected edge silently rescue a directed-hinge bifurcation that the notes' "both endnodes have arrowheads" addition is designed to exclude.
